title: Tapping to Fall Asleep in Belonging
subtitle: A nighttime tapping practice for releasing the loneliness of night and settling into connection
excerpt: A five-minute nighttime tapping practice from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script addresses the experience of night as separation — the stillness that can tip into loneliness. The reframe moves toward sleep as an act of rejoining something larger: the rhythm of the natural world that is also resting.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 27 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed when the quiet of night feels isolating, or when falling asleep feels like being cut off from the world rather than held by it.

content.text: A five-minute nighttime tapping practice from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the loneliness that can come with night: the stillness that reads as separation, the quiet that feels like being left behind. The reframe moves toward the specific felt sense that the rest of the living world is also exhaling — and you are part of that.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though night sometimes feels lonely, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though stillness can feel like separation, I honour the truth that I'm always connected.
Even though sleep feels like letting go, I'm open to seeing it as returning home.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: The world outside is resting too.
Side of eye: The trees, the air, the stars — all exhaling.
Under eye: I'm part of something bigger when I rest.
Under nose: Stillness unites us, not divides us.
Chin: I'm never alone in the rhythm of night.
Collarbone: My body joins the earth in its exhale.
Under arm: I'm part of this natural peace.
Top of head: I fall asleep held by that belonging.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I sleep as part of something whole.
Side of eye: I'm connected even in silence.
Under eye: My breath joins the world's breath.
Under nose: Night embraces me completely.
Chin: Rest connects me to everything good.
Collarbone: I'm one with the peace around me.
Under arm: I fall asleep surrounded by safety.
Top of head: I rest deeply, held and part of it all.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 27 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
